==Biography==
Alberto "'''Crumbz'''" Rengifo is from Venezuela and currently lives in the United States. He began playing League of Legends in August 2010.

===Season 1===
In March 2011, Crumbzz joined the gaming clan [[DSO]] as their top laner. He left team DSO in mid April to join [[Team Godmode]] with [[h4ckerv2]], [[illustrious3]], [[kenikth]], [[xxPhaxen]], and [[samsterlicious]]. Crumbzz substituted for as the midlaner for [[Epik Gamer]] at [[IGN Proleague Season 3 - Atlantic City|IPL 3]] on October 6 when the team's starting midlaner [[Salce]] was unable to participate. The team secured second place in the tournament, beating [[v8 Esports]] 2-0 and then [[Team SoloMid]] 2-1 before losing 2-1 to [[Team Dignitas]] in the finals.

Immediately after IPL 3, Crumbzz accepted an offer from [[Curse Gaming]] to become their new jungler, replacing [[Lapaka]]. With Curse, Crumbzz established himself as a top North American jungler. As one of the 8 teams invited to the [[MLG 2011 - Providence|MLG Providence]] qualifiers, Curse Gaming started off strongly by defeating [[Team Space Monkeys]] 2-0 in the Round of 32 and then [[Leaguecraft Gaming]] 2-0 in the Round of 16; however in the quarterfinals they once again lost to Dignitas 2-1 and were knocked out.

===Season 2===
In Season 2, Crumbzz along with Team Curse achieved multiple 5th/6th-place results at [[IEM Season VI - Global Challenge Kiev|IEM Kiev]] and [[IEM Season VI - World Championship|IEM Hanover]], and then [[IGN Proleague Season 4 - Las Vegas|IPL4]]. In May 2012, Crumbzz announced that he was leaving Curse due to his inability to move into their gaming house.<ref>[http://na.leagueoflegends.com/board/showthread.php?t=2133081 Crumbzz statement, leaving Curse Gaming] ''na.leagueoflegends.com''</ref> Curse acquired [[Saintvicious]] from [[CLG]] as a replacement, and Dignitas's [[Voyboy]] moved to CLG; this left a space on Dignitas, and Crumbzz joined as their new toplaner, switching roles from jungle. Proving his ability to play multiple roles at a high level, Crumbzz helped Dignitas achieve a 3rd-place finish at the [[MLG Pro Circuit - Spring Championship|MLG Anaheim]].<ref>[http://s3.majorleaguegaming.com/2012-anaheim-leagueoflegends-champ.html 2012 MLG Anaheim League of Legends Brackets] ''s3.majorleaguegaming.com''</ref>

===Preseason 3===
After [[I Will Dominate]] was banned from professional League of Legends play for one year and left Dignitas<ref>[http://team-dignitas.net/articles/news/League-of-Legends/2605/Regarding-the-I-Will-Dominate-Ruling Regarding the I Will Dominate Ruling]</ref>, Crumbzz switched from toplane to jungle on December 7, 2012.

===Season 3===
[[Team Dignitas|Dignitas]] ended the Season 3 [[Riot_League_Championship_Series/North_America/Season_3/Spring_Round_Robin|NA LCS spring split]] season in third place, advancing to the playoffs but then losing 1-2 to [[Good Game University]]. They beat [[Team Summon]] in [[Riot League Championship Series/North America/Season 3/Summer Promotion|relegations]] and qualified for the [[Riot_League_Championship_Series/North_America/Season_3/Summer_Round_Robin|summer split]], where they finished fifth in the round robin and then fourth overall, avoiding relegation. They placed fourth in the [[Riot_League_Championship_Series/North_America/Season_3/Summer_Playoffs|summer playoffs]].

Crumbzz was a member of the [[Season 3 World Championship]] analyst desk, along with [[Nien]] and [[ZionSpartan]].<ref>[http://na.lolesports.com/articles/casters-and-analysts-announced-worlds Casters and analysts announced for Worlds] - ''lolesports''</ref>

===2014 Season===
[[Team Dignitas|Dignitas]] ended the [[Riot_League_Championship_Series/North_America/2014_Season/Spring_Round_Robin|spring split]] in fourth sixth place. In the [[Riot_League_Championship_Series/North_America/2014_Season/Spring_Playoffs|playoffs]], they lost to [[Team Curse]] in the quarterfinals and then beat [[Team Coast]] to avoid [[Riot_League_Championship_Series/North_America/2014_Season/Summer_Promotion|relegation]]. In the [[Riot_League_Championship_Series/North_America/2014_Season/Summer_Round_Robin|summer split]], they placed sixth and then lost in the quarterfinals of the [[Riot League Championship Series/North America/2014 Season/Summer Playoffs|playoffs]] to [[Team Solo Mid]] 1-3; once again, however, they avoided [[Riot League Championship Series/North America/2015 Season/Spring Promotion|relegation]], this time beating CLG.

On July 15, Crumbzz appeared on [[Summoning Insight]] and discussed the current state of Dignitas among other topics.<ref>[http://www.ongamers.com/articles/summoning-insight-episode-16-vod-with-special-gues/1100-1946/ Summoning Insight Episode 16 VOD, with special guest Crumbzz] - OnGamers</ref> Crumbzz was also a member of the Analyst Desk at [[2014 Season World Championship|Worlds 2014]] and on several occasions correctly predicted the underdog team to win despite the other three members of the desk disagreeing with him. As a result, the Worlds production team put together a video of Crumbzz dressed up as the wizard "Professor Crumbledore" predicting that [[Samsung White]] would win Worlds.<ref>[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mwb-11MTp3A Crumbz the False Prophet predicts S4 LoL World Championship Semi Final and Final Winners]</ref>

===2015 Preseason===
Despite a rumor claiming that Crumbzz was retiring, on Friday October 24, 2014 Dignitas announced that Crumbzz had re-signed with the team as their jungler and team captain after the expiration of his previous contract.<ref>[http://team-dignitas.net/articles/news/League-of-Legends/6102/Crumbz-signs-as-Team-Dignitas-Jungler-and-Captain Crumbzz signs as Team Dignitas Jungler and Captain] - ''team-dignitas.net''</ref>

===2015 Season===
In February 2015, Crumbzz stepped down from Team Dignitas.<ref>[http://team-dignitas.net/articles/news/League-of-Legends/6587/Crumbz-steps-down-from-Team-Dignitas Crumbz steps down from Team Dignitas] ''teamdignitas.net''</ref> He later formed a Challenger team called [[Misfits (North American Team)|Misfits]], along with [[Alex Ich]] and [[Remilia]]. [[RF Legendary]] and [[Intense]] later joined after a tryout.<ref>[http://www.thescoreesports.com/lol/news/671 Alex Ich & Crumbzz to form Challenger team] - ' 'thescoreesports.com' '</ref> Misfits won the [[AlphaDraft Challenger League]] and then participated in was the [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Summer Qualifier|NACS Summer Qualifier]], where they dropped only one game in their three series to qualify for the [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Summer Season|Summer Season]]. After qualifying, Misfits rebranded as '''Renegades'''.

Renegades finished the Challenger Series regular season with an 8-2 record and in second place, after losing a tiebreaker to [[Team Coast]]. However, in the [[2015 NA Challenger Series/Summer Playoffs|playoffs]], they defeated [[Team Imagine|Imagine]] 2-1 and then Coast 3-2, sending them directly to the [[League Championship Series/North America/2016 Season/Spring Season|2016 NA LCS Spring Season]].

=== 2016 Season ===
Renegades was faced with significant roster instability in the spring season, as first AD carry [[Freeze]] was unable to play for three games early on in the split, and then Remilia stepped down one game after his return. RF Legendary was also replaced by [[Flaresz]] for three games later on. After the seventh week of play, Renegades and [[Team Dragon Knights]] traded several players, with [[Seraph]] and [[Ninja]] joining Renegades as top and mid laner, respectively. Crumbz had also substituted for TDK in the final week of the [[NA Challenger Series/2016 Season/Spring Season|NACS Spring Season]]. This roster for Renegades finally proved successful, but it was too little too late, and the team was only able to improve to 8th place in the league, after winning a tiebreaker against [[Team Impulse]]. In the [[League Championship Series/North America/2016 Season/Summer Promotion|Summer Promotion Tournament]], Renegades beat TDK 3-0 and qualified for the [[League Championship Series/North America/2016 Season/Summer Season|summer season]].

However, on May 8, Riot released a [[List of Competitive Rulings|competitive ruling]] stating that Renegades would be forced to sell their LCS seed due to multiple rule violations, and Chris Badawi would be permanently banned from "association or affiliation with any team or organization participating in a Riot-sanctioned league".<ref name="comprulingmay2016">[http://www.lolesports.com/en_US/articles/competitive-ruling-renegades-and-tdk Competitive Ruling: Renegades and TDK] ''lolesports.com''</ref> Crumbz departed the team a few days later and was named as a coach of [[Apex Gaming]] and their sister team [[Apex Pride]]. He also appeared on Riot's broadcasts frequently during the summer split.

== Trivia ==
* Favorite video game is Little Big Adventure. 2 <ref>[http://clgaming.net/forums/board39-reign-of-gaming/board40-blogs/board63-crumbzz/3198-summoner-spells-for-better-or-worse-lba2/ Summoner Spells For Better or Worse + LBA2] ''clgaming.net''</ref>
* Ranked #1 on the NA Solo 5v5 ladder on numerous occasions.
* Infamous for his dominant {{ci|Shaco}} play.
* Ranked on the top 5 of the NA Solo 5v5 ladder with two different accounts simultaneously.
* Favorite champion is {{ci|Jax}}.
* Maintains a popular [http://grooveshark.com/#!/crumbzzz/music Grooveshark playlist].
* Predicted several upsets at the [[Season 4 World Championship]] earning him the title "Crumbzz the Prophet."
* He has played Mid Lane, Top Lane and Jungler competitively over his professional career.
* Plays the saxophone.
* Has worked as a sailing instructor.<ref>[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iYEROKJVprg My New Start Coaching] ''youtube.com''</ref>
{{PlayerStatsTrivia}}
